Explore by Region

A Land of a Thousand Hills — Six Distinct Worlds

Each corridor tells a different story. Open one and step into its world.

terrainExplore the Highlands

Northern Corridor — Volcanoes National Park

The heart of Rwanda's conservation efforts and the majestic home of the mountain gorillas.

add
Iconic Experience

Gorilla Trekking

Witness the legendary Mountain Gorillas in their natural habitat. Rwanda currently has 12 habituated gorilla families available to visit. The annual "Kwita Izina" baby gorilla naming ceremony, held since 2005, is rooted in a centuries-old Rwandan tradition. Only 8 people per gorilla family per day, visits capped at one hour, minimum age of 15.

  • check_circleMaintain 10m distance
  • check_circleMaximum 1 hour observation
  • check_circleNo flash photography
From $1,500
Book This Expedition

Karisimbi Hiking

Climb the highest of the Virunga volcanoes at 4,507m for a challenging multi-day trek.

Learn More arrow_forward

Bisoke Hiking

Ascend to the stunning crater lake at the summit of Mount Bisoke.

Learn More arrow_forward

Gahinga Hiking

Explore the extinct volcano with its unique bamboo forest ecosystem.

Learn More arrow_forward

Sabyinyo Climbing

Trek to the "Old Man's Teeth" — a challenging climb across three countries.

Learn More arrow_forward

Iby'Iwacu Cultural Village

Immerse yourself in traditional Rwandan village life and customs.

Learn More arrow_forward

Gorilla Museum

Learn about conservation history and gorilla research at the base of the volcanoes.

Learn More arrow_forward

Coffee Farm Tour Experience

Visit local coffee plantations and learn the crop-to-cup process.

Learn More arrow_forward

Twin Lakes

Visit Lake Burera and Lake Ruhondo — two stunning crater lakes with breathtaking views.

Learn More arrow_forward
View All Northern Corridor Packages →
museumSouthern History

Southern Corridor

A deep dive into Rwanda's royal history, cultural heritage, and the somber lessons of Murambi.

add

King's Palace

Visit the restored royal palace in Nyanza, the seat of the ancient Rwandan monarchy.

Learn More arrow_forward

Ethnographic Museum

Explore one of Africa's finest ethnographic museums in Huye, showcasing Rwandan cultural artifacts.

Learn More arrow_forward

Coffee Tour Experience

Travel to the southern coffee-growing regions for a unique agrotourism experience.

Learn More arrow_forward

Murambi Genocide Memorial

A dignified place of remembrance. We approach this visit with profound respect.

Learn More arrow_forward
View All Southern Corridor Packages →
forestAncient Rainforest

Nyungwe Forest National Park

One of Africa's oldest rainforests, home to 13 primate species and the famous canopy walkway.

add

Chimpanzee Trekking

Track habituated chimpanzee troops through the ancient rainforest.

Learn More arrow_forward

Nyungwe Guided Nature Walks

Explore diverse trails through one of Africa's oldest rainforests.

Learn More arrow_forward

Nyungwe Bird Watching

Spot over 300 bird species, including 26 endemics to the Albertine Rift.

Learn More arrow_forward

Nyungwe Canopy Walk

Walk among the clouds on Africa's highest canopy walkway, suspended 50m above the forest floor.

Learn More arrow_forward

Tropical Waterfall Guided Hike

Hike through dense forest to discover stunning waterfalls.

Learn More arrow_forward

Congo Nile Divide Guided Hike

Trek along the continental divide separating the Congo and Nile basins.

Learn More arrow_forward
View All Nyungwe Forest Packages →
sailingScenic Drive

Kivu Belt Road

The most beautiful road in Rwanda, winding along the sapphire waters and emerald islands of Lake Kivu.

add

Boat Experience on Lake Kivu

Sunset cruises and island-hopping adventures from Karongi and Rubavu.

Learn More arrow_forward

Island Visits on Lake Kivu

Explore the serene islands of Lake Kivu from Karongi.

Learn More arrow_forward
View All Kivu Belt Road Packages →
safariSavannah Wilderness

Eastern Province — Akagera National Park

Savannah safaris in Rwanda's Big Five national park. Boat trips on Lake Ihema.

add

Akagera National Park

Witness the Big Five in Central Africa's largest protected wetland. Game drives and boat safaris available.

Learn More arrow_forward
View All Eastern Province Packages →
location_cityRemembrance & Reflection

Kigali & Historical Legacies

Remembrance, reflection, and the vibrant culture of Africa's cleanest city.

add

Kigali Genocide Memorial

A place of dignified remembrance. Guided tours are conducted respectfully.

Learn More arrow_forward

Richard Kandt Museum

The former home of Rwanda's first German resident, now a natural history museum.

Learn More arrow_forward

Liberation Museum

Learn about Rwanda's post-genocide reconstruction and reconciliation journey.

Learn More arrow_forward

Nyandungu Eco Park

A restored urban wetland and eco-park perfect for nature walks within Kigali.

Learn More arrow_forward

Kigali City Tour

Explore Kigali's vibrant markets, art galleries, and world-class coffee scene.

Learn More arrow_forward
View All Kigali City Packages →
Ready to Explore?

Tell Us About Your Visit

Whether you're interested in gorilla trekking, historical memorials, or a scenic drive along the Kivu Belt, our team is here to curate your perfect Rwandan journey.
